db_assertions: a SECOND tenant (org+project+admin key) was created through POST /v1/organizations|projects|api-keys on the SAME running control-plane process; the key is plaintext only in the create response and hashed at rest (000030 scope/expires_at)
PATCH /v1/projects/{id} wrote a config_policy the §14 resolver observed (allowed_models/default_tools took on the project layer) — the config_policy write-path is API-reachable for the first time

restart-less spine: the eight SPINE steps (provision-org/project/api-key, config-policy, real-run, steer, list-history, cross-tenant-deny) resolved on ONE process boot with restart_count=0 (proven live by pg_postmaster_start_time identical start-to-end); the finer MCI steps (secret/artifact/budget/route/binding) are SEPARATE live smokes in their own processes. journey_digest = hashParts(canonical step_ids) is re-derivable + anchored (anti-fabrication)
POST /v1/secret-refs wrote a write-only value, then a rotate inserted version 2 (append-only secret_refs, 000031) — no UPDATE/DELETE grant, so the append-only history holds across reboots

the run resolved the secret through the DB-backed resolver ahead of the env-file bridge with NO control-plane restart (restart_count=0); the plaintext value never appeared in a response, log, or event (value_surfaced=false)
honest ceiling: at-rest protection is a single master-key AES-GCM envelope; the KMS/lease ceremony is E13-H (SEC-001/003)
GET /v1/responses returned tenant B's OWN run history through an opaque tenant-bound cursor; tenant B's page disclosed ZERO of tenant A's runs (leaked_rows=0)
cross-tenant negative: tenant B's GET of tenant A's run id returned 404 (existence non-disclosure) and a foreign cursor was rejected — the deny is a real status, not a log line
the isolation is DB-enforced: RLS FORCE + the per-transaction palai.org_id GUC (000029) means a WHERE-less query sees no tenant rows (TEN-001/002 corpus)

the run produced a workspace artifact; GET /v1/artifacts/{id}/content streamed it and the RFC 9530 Content-Digest matched sha256 of the downloaded bytes bit-for-bit (digest_matches=true, re-derivable from the artifact bytes)
cross-tenant negative: tenant B's GET of tenant A's artifact id returned 404 (existence non-disclosure), zero bytes leaked (leaked_rows=0)
honest ceiling: authenticated direct download; the pre-signed URL policy + expiry ceremony is E13-H (DAT-006 remainder)
a burst past the per-API-key request-rate bucket got 429 + Retry-After + RFC9457; the refused request never reached admission, so no billable compute started (§20.12)
the durable-budget half (a low-budget project's second run refused with 402) is proven at BIL-001/QUO-001's tier; this case pins the edge rate refusal
honest ceiling: in-process token bucket — correct for the single-replica compose; a distributed limiter + weighted fairness (QUO-002) is SaaS scope

two projects on ONE stack resolved DISTINCT published model routes: project A -> gpt-4o-mini, project B -> gpt-4o, each with its own model_connection credential (secret-ref backed) — the DB-backed reader chose model+credential per project, not the env default
the env route (PALAI_MODEL_PROVIDER/MODEL) fell to the deployment-default lowest layer; it was not the source of either project's model id
honest ceiling: ONE provider family (provider-one) — a second independent adapter + capability probe is E16; this proves per-project model+credential selection only
a repository binding whose connection_ref was set resolved a BINDING-SCOPED Git credential through the secret-ref path (T3), not the global GitHub App fallback (resolved_via_ref=true)
a ref-less binding still resolves through the global App fallback (unchanged) — the seam is additive
honest ceiling: the connection_ref resolver SEAM only; a per-tenant GitHub App onboarding surface is out of scope (SaaS/product)

a session was created, a run admitted, and a steer command (send_message) posted through POST /v1/sessions/{id}/commands over the SAME public HTTP API the @palai/sdk targets (raw HTTP in the Go journey) — the E08 durable command spine is reached over the public API; the command was durably ACCEPTED (202) and carried its command id (applied=accepted, not yet loop-boundary-applied)

the @palai/sdk client shape for this surface (sessions.commands.steer/interrupt, artifacts.download, server-only credential guard) is proven by its own vitest suite (sdks/typescript/test/resources.test.ts) against mock fetch — NO test connects the SDK to a live server, so this case claims the HTTP surface, not an SDK-to-server round-trip
honest ceilings: the steer's loop-boundary APPLICATION is the e2e proof (TestSteerAppliesAtNextLoopBoundaryWithSequence, MCI-008 catalog); Py/Go SDK parity is E16
